criteria_verification.evidence: Added to_connect_kwargs() method to NATSConfig (config.py lines 153-178). Method builds a dict with servers (as list), connect_timeout, reconnect_time_wait, max_reconnect_attempts, and name. Conditionally includes user+password (unwrapped from SecretStr) when both are set, and credentials when creds_file is set. Verified by 12 tests in TestToConnectKwargsSmoke, TestToConnectKwargsKeyExample, and TestToConnectKwargsEdgeCase classes — all passing.
criteria_verification.notes: Added to_connect_kwargs() method to NATSConfig (config.py lines 153-178). Method builds a dict with servers (as list), connect_timeout, reconnect_time_wait, max_reconnect_attempts, and name. Conditionally includes user+password (unwrapped from SecretStr) when both are set, and credentials when creds_file is set. Verified by 12 tests in TestToConnectKwargsSmoke, TestToConnectKwargsKeyExample, and TestToConnectKwargsEdgeCase classes — all passing.

criteria_verification.criterion_text: `model_dump()` does not expose raw password string — `SecretStr` serialises as `"**********"` by default in pydantic v2
criteria_verification.evidence: Verified by TestSerialisationMasking::test_model_dump_password_is_masked and pre-existing TestNATSConfigAuthBoundary::test_password_not_exposed_in_model_dump. Both confirm 'super-secret' does not appear in model_dump() output. This is pydantic v2 built-in SecretStr behaviour — no implementation changes needed, just test verification.
criteria_verification.notes: Verified by TestSerialisationMasking::test_model_dump_password_is_masked and pre-existing TestNATSConfigAuthBoundary::test_password_not_exposed_in_model_dump. Both confirm 'super-secret' does not appear in model_dump() output. This is pydantic v2 built-in SecretStr behaviour — no implementation changes needed, just test verification.

criteria_verification.criterion_text: `repr(config)` / `str(config)` does not expose raw password value
criteria_verification.evidence: Verified by TestSerialisationMasking::test_repr_password_is_masked and TestSerialisationMasking::test_str_password_is_masked. Both confirm 'super-secret' does not appear in repr() or str() output. Pre-existing test TestNATSConfigAuthBoundary::test_password_masked_in_repr also confirms this. Pydantic v2 SecretStr handles masking automatically.
criteria_verification.notes: Verified by TestSerialisationMasking::test_repr_password_is_masked and TestSerialisationMasking::test_str_password_is_masked. Both confirm 'super-secret' does not appear in repr() or str() output. Pre-existing test TestNATSConfigAuthBoundary::test_password_masked_in_repr also confirms this. Pydantic v2 SecretStr handles masking automatically.

criteria_verification.criterion_text: `to_connect_kwargs()` output is accepted by nats-py `Client.connect(**kwargs)` (verified in integration test)
criteria_verification.evidence: Added TestToConnectKwargsIntegration::test_kwargs_accepted_by_nats_client_connect marked with @pytest.mark.integration. The test inspects the Client.connect() signature and verifies all keys from to_connect_kwargs() are accepted parameters. Uses pytest.skip if nats-py is not installed. This does not require a running NATS server.
